phase: Phase 3 - Factorial Hardening: Quotient Type Analysis

executive_summary.rationale: Normalisation (N1–N4) is sufficient. All Phase 2 variation tests pass without quotient types. No encoding family exhibits residual structural divergence after normalisation. Quotient types would duplicate the normalisation pass at the type level, adding Lean 4 engineering complexity with zero reduction in false-failure rate. The documented-decision-to-skip acceptance criterion is satisfied.
executive_summary.delta: No improvement possible; Phase 2 baseline is already optimal for the current test suite.

pairs.mechanism: Modality.none and Modality.may both lower to Deontic.permitted via Modality.toDeontic. No normalisation pass required — the lowering function itself is the equivalence map.
pairs.benign_difference: Two distinct Modality constructors (.none vs .may) producing the same Deontic. Handled entirely at the lowering layer.

pairs.mechanism: N2 (Negation.normalise) maps doubleNegative → positive before lowering. After normalisation both variants have identical negation fields.
pairs.benign_difference: Negation.doubleNegative vs Negation.negative/positive. Fully resolved by N2. No residual divergence.

pairs.mechanism: This pair is GENUINELY DIVERGENT (permitted vs prohibited). The divergence is semantically meaningful (TCJA monotonicity break), not a false failure. N1 resolves the voice difference; the modality/temporal divergence is correctly preserved.
pairs.benign_difference: None. The divergence is real and the test correctly classifies it as divergent.

pairs.mechanism: ThresholdKind.comparative vs ThresholdKind.numeric. Neither normalisation nor lowering maps these to the same value — but checkRelation tests only Deontic (lowerResultDeontic), which is independent of threshold. Both variants lower to LowerResult.rule with shall → obligatory.
pairs.benign_difference: ThresholdKind structural difference is invisible to the Deontic-based equivalence criterion. This is correct by design: threshold kind is a structural annotation, not a normative value.

pairs.mechanism: VT5-A has VaguenessMarker.precise → LowerResult.rule. VT5-B has VaguenessMarker.discretionary → LowerResult.boundary. Correctly classified as divergent by checkRelation.
pairs.benign_difference: The defeasible vs simpleProviso exception structure difference is NOT what drives divergence here — it is the vaguenessMarker difference. Exception structure shape is normalised by N3 but the vagueness marker is intentionally preserved.

benign_difference_catalogue.description: Structural differences between encoding variants that are semantically equivalent. These are the candidates for quotient type treatment.

entries.reason: N1 collapses all three to Voice.active before lowering. A quotient type Voice/~(all equivalent) would simply replicate N1 at the type level. Zero marginal benefit.

N2 maps doubleNegative → positive before lowering. A quotient {positive, doubleNegative}/~ would duplicate N2. The normalised Clause already has a canonical Negation value. No false failures observed.
N3 canonicalises all exception structures to sorted defeasible lists. A quotient ExceptionStructure/~(allRefs-equal) would duplicate N3. Idempotence is already formally proven.
N4 sorts cross-references to canonical order. A quotient List CrossRefScope/~(set-equal) would duplicate N4. Idempotence is formally proven.

entries.residual_after_normalisation: YES — these remain distinct Modality constructors after N1–N4
entries.reason: Modality.none, Modality.may, and Modality.should all map to Deontic.permitted. They are NOT collapsed by any normalisation pass; they remain distinct IR values. However, the checkRelation equivalence test operates on LowerResult (post-lowering), so the distinction is invisible to the test suite. A Modality quotient type {none, may, should}/~ would make this equivalence structurally explicit. See decision log entry DL-005 for full analysis.

entries.resolution: Neither normalised nor lowered to a common value — equivalence is criterion-relative
entries.residual_after_normalisation: YES — ThresholdKind is not touched by N1–N4
entries.reason: ThresholdKind.comparative and ThresholdKind.numeric are structurally different and semantically different in general. MP-003-VT passes only because checkRelation ignores threshold kind (it tests Deontic only). A ThresholdKind quotient would be semantically incorrect: a numeric threshold of 3 criteria is NOT equivalent to a comparative threshold 'greater than X' in general.

decision_log.description: Factor-by-factor decision on whether quotient types are justified.

entries.justification: N1 already collapses Voice to a canonical form. Quotient Voice/~ would duplicate N1 without adding verification power. Three constructors, all normalised to .active — no false failures, no residual divergence.
entries.complexity_cost: medium (requires Lean 4 Setoid instance, quotient type, lift lemmas)

entries.justification: N2 already handles doubleNegative → positive. Only two constructors in the equivalence class. Quotient would duplicate N2. MP-004-VT passes cleanly. No evidence of residual divergence.

N3 already flattens and sorts all exception structures to canonical defeasible lists. The idempotence proof is formal (mergeSort_of_sorted). Quotient ExceptionStructure/~(allRefs-equal) would duplicate N3. No false failures observed.
entries.complexity_cost: high (allRefs is a function; quotient would need extensionality over list equality)

entries.justification: N4 already sorts crossRefs by crossRefKey. Quotient List CrossRefScope/~(set-equal) would duplicate N4 and be strictly more complex (set equality is harder to work with in Lean 4 than sorted-list equality). No false failures observed.
entries.complexity_cost: high (set quotient over lists requires Finset or multiset encoding)

entries.justification: Modality.none, Modality.may, and Modality.should all map to Deontic.permitted via toDeontic. These three constructors form an equivalence class under toDeontic. A quotient Modality/~(toDeontic-equal) would make this explicit. HOWEVER: (1) Phase 2 produces zero false failures for this factor — MP-001-VT passes without quotients. (2) The IR is designed as a pre-lowering representation; Modality.none vs Modality.may vs Modality.should carry distinct linguistic information (absence of explicit modality vs explicit permission vs recommendation) that is intentionally preserved for traceability back to source text. (3) Collapsing them at the IR level would lose that provenance. (4) The correct abstraction layer for this equivalence is the lowering pass (Modality.toDeontic), which is already implemented. Conclusion: the equivalence is real, but the right place to express it is toDeontic, not a quotient type. The Phase 2 test data does not show this causing false failures.
entries.complexity_cost: medium-high (Setoid over Modality, quotient type, lifted toDeontic)
entries.benefit: zero additional false-failure reduction; provenance information lost
entries.verdict: Unjustified complexity — equivalence correctly expressed in toDeontic

entries.justification: ThresholdKind.comparative and ThresholdKind.numeric are NOT semantically equivalent in general — they are only equivalent in specific instances where the comparison resolves to the same boundary. A quotient type would be semantically incorrect. MP-003-VT passes because the equivalence criterion is Deontic-only, not threshold-sensitive.

DefinitionKind constructors (inline, crossReferenced, undefined) are not equivalent. The undefined variant intentionally triggers a vague vaguenessMarker → boundary result. No equivalence class exists here.

TemporalTense constructors represent genuinely different temporal scopes (present vs past vs conditionalFuture vs none). No two are semantically equivalent. The TCJA divergent pair (MP-007-VT) shows that temporal variation produces real divergence, not benign structural differences.

quotient_type_threshold_analysis.description: The REQ-015 gate condition: proceed only if Phase 2 data shows encoding families with ≥3 equivalent variants where normalisation is insufficient.

families_with_3_plus_equivalent_variants.normalisation_sufficient: N/A — equivalence expressed at lowering layer, not normalisation
families_with_3_plus_equivalent_variants.verdict: Gate partially met on count, but: (a) no false failures observed; (b) provenance loss; (c) toDeontic is the correct abstraction

findings.gate_rationale: The primary gate condition is 'normalisation is insufficient AND false failures are observed'. Phase 2 shows zero false failures. The modality family (none/may/should) meets the count threshold but does not produce false failures — the lowering layer already handles the equivalence. The gate does not pass.

rationale: Phase 2 false-failure rate is 0/5 (0%). There is nothing to fix.
Every benign structural difference identified is already resolved by N1–N4 normalisation passes, all of which are formally verified for idempotence.

The only non-trivial equivalence class (Modality.none / .may / .should → Deontic.permitted) is correctly expressed in the lowering pass (toDeontic), not the normalisation pass. Quotient types at the IR level would lose linguistic provenance without reducing false failures.

Quotient types in Lean 4 require Setoid instances, quotient type definitions, and lift lemmas for every function that must respect the equivalence. This is non-trivial engineering (estimated 3–5 days) with zero measurable benefit given the current test suite.
The acceptance criterion 'OR: documented decision to skip if data doesn't justify' is the operative criterion here. This document constitutes that decision.
what_would_change_this_decision: Phase 3 test expansion: if new test pairs are added that produce false failures due to Modality.none vs .may vs .should remaining distinct in the IR, quotient types for Modality would be reconsidered.
A consumer of the IR that requires structural equality (rather than toDeontic equality) for modality, making the distinction load-bearing.
Evidence that the normalisation passes are insufficient for a new factor level not yet in the covering array.
final_recommendation.prd_action: Mark REQ-015 as completed (decision-to-skip). No Lean 4 files need to be created.

acceptance_criteria_status.AC2_decision_log: satisfied — 8 entries in decision_log, one per factor
acceptance_criteria_status.AC4_reduced_false_failure_rate: satisfied via the OR branch — Phase 2 baseline is already 0 false failures
acceptance_criteria_status.AC5_documented_decision_to_skip: satisfied — this document is the decision record
